seo.title: Falls Prevention for Senior Living & CCRCs | Able Care
seo.description: Objective falls screening for CCRCs, rental retirement, independent and assisted living. Protect residents, reduce staff turnover, and avoid falls-related liability.

blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Older man at a senior living community using Able Assess
blocks.headline: Protect residents. Reduce staff turnover. Avoid falls-related liability.
blocks.subtitle: Objective falls screening for CCRCs, rental retirement, independent and assisted living. Standardized across every level of care.

blocks.heading: Technology built for the frontline
blocks.body: Able Assess fits into the resident's daily routine. Five minutes in the wellness center, the dining room, or the resident's apartment. Non-clinical staff can run it. Results are available immediately.
blocks.imageAlt: Older man at a senior living community during an Able Assess screening

blocks.heading: How it works in senior living

steps.title: Capture
steps.description: A wellness director or other staff member runs a five-minute assessment during a routine wellness check.

steps.title: Score
steps.description: Grip strength, sit-to-stand, gait speed and timed up-and-go are scored against normative data instantly.

steps.title: Act
steps.description: Outcome report is available immediately and can be used for referrals to physical therapy, updated in the care plan, or sent to families to improve communication.

steps.title: Track
steps.description: Longitudinal trends give residents objective milestones and give operators population-level insight into fall risk.

blocks.heading: Four metrics, one picture of function

metrics.name: Grip strength
metrics.description: Full body strength proxy. Predicts frailty, mortality and functional decline.

metrics.name: Sit-to-stand
metrics.description: Lower-limb power and balance. Key to independent mobility in IL and AL.

metrics.name: Gait speed
metrics.description: Walking speed over a standardized 4-metre distance. Linked to fall risk and level-of-care transitions.

metrics.name: Timed Up and Go
metrics.description: Captures mobility, balance and transfer ability in a single measure. Validated across community and clinical settings.

blocks.heading: The numbers that matter to senior living

stats.label: Reduction in falls with objective screening
Clinical certifications required to run it

testimonials.quote: It paid for itself in the first quarter.
Residents can see their own progress. That changes the conversation with families entirely.
testimonials.attribution: Wellness Director, US senior living community

blocks.heading: Common questions from senior living operators

items.question: What hardware do we need?
items.answer: The sensor, pouch and measuring tape are all provided as part of the kit. It pairs via Bluetooth with any smartphone or tablet. No additional infrastructure required.

items.question: Can non-clinical staff run the assessment?
items.answer: Yes, Able Assess was built for clinical and non-clinical staff. Wellness directors, care aides and activities coordinators can all run the protocol with no training course.

items.question: How does it help with occupancy?
items.answer: Falls are a leading cause of resident turnover. Objective screening has been shown to reduce falls by 25%, which protects length of stay and occupancy rates.

items.question: Can residents see their own data?
items.answer: Yes. Residents get objective milestones they can track over time, which motivates participation in wellness programs and builds trust in the care plan.

items.question: How does it reduce liability?
items.answer: A standardized, documented screening protocol creates a defensible record of proactive falls risk management for every resident.

blocks.heading: See Able Assess in your community.
blocks.body: Book a presentation with a clinician who has run it in senior living.
